ucs.title: [LLM] Salt Typhoon (Earth Estries / GhostEmperor) LSASS credential theft via rundll32 + comsvcs.dll MiniDump
ucs.description: Hunts the in-memory LSASS-dump tradecraft repeatedly attributed to Salt Typhoon / Earth Estries by Trend Micro and Microsoft (Volt/Salt-Typhoon series): rundll32.exe invoking the MiniDump export of comsvcs.dll against the lsass.exe PID and dropping the output to a staging directory before exfil via Impacket/PuTTY pscp. Distinctive because Salt Typhoon prefers this LOLBin chain over Mimikatz to avoid AV signatures on telecom and government boxes.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" (Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll*MiniDump*" OR Processes.process="*comsvcs.dll,#24*" OR Processes.process="*comsvcs*MiniDumpW*") NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_id Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=cmdline "(?i)comsvcs\.dll[,\s]+(?<export>#?24|MiniDump\w*)\s+(?<targetPid>\d+)\s+(?<dumpPath>\S+)" | eval salt_typhoon_uc="comsvcs_minidump" | where isnotnull(targetPid) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where AccountName !in~ ("system","local service","network service")
// Salt Typhoon canonical pattern: rundll32 comsvcs.dll, MiniDump <pid> <dump> full
| where ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs"
   and (ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDump"
        or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)comsvcs\.dll[,\s]+#?24\s+\d+")
| extend TargetPid = extract(@"(?i)(?:MiniDump\w*|#?24)\s+(\d+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| extend DumpPath = extract(@"(?i)(?:MiniDump\w*|#?24)\s+\d+\s+([^\s\"]+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          IsRemoteSession = InitiatingProcessTokenElevation,
          ProcessCommandLine, TargetPid, DumpPath, SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\rundll32.exe"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where SubjectUserName !in~ ("SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE")
| where CommandLine has "comsvcs"
   and (CommandLine has "MiniDump"
        or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)comsvcs\.dll[,\s]+#?24\s+\d+")
| extend TargetPid = extract(@"(?i)(?:MiniDump\w*|#?24)\s+(\d+)", 1, CommandLine)
| extend DumpPath  = extract(@"(?i)(?:MiniDump\w*|#?24)\s+\d+\s+([^\s\"]+)", 1, CommandLine)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName,
          NewProcessName, CommandLine, TargetPid, DumpPath, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Salt Typhoon LSASS Credential Dump via comsvcs.dll MiniDump
id: 2f9d4a18-1c1a-4f3d-9d6c-saltyphoon01
status: experimental
description: Detects rundll32.exe invoking comsvcs.dll!MiniDump (ordinal #24) to dump LSASS, a TTP repeatedly attributed to Salt Typhoon / Earth Estries / GhostEmperor by Trend Micro and Microsoft.
references:
  - https://www.trendmicro.com/en_us/research/24/k/earth-estries.html
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1045/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.t1218.011
  - detection.salt_typhoon
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_img:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
  selection_cmd:
    CommandLine|contains: 'comsvcs'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'MiniDump'
      - ',#24 '
      - ', #24 '
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  filter_system:
    User|contains:
      - 'SYSTEM'
      - 'LOCAL SERVICE'
      - 'NETWORK SERVICE'
  condition: selection_img and selection_cmd and not (filter_machine or filter_system)
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate IR / forensic dumping by SOC tools (rare; baseline by host)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Salt Typhoon (G1045 / Earth Estries / GhostEmperor / FamousSparrow) is documented by Trend Micro and Microsoft using exactly this rundll32 → comsvcs.dll MiniDump pattern against telecom and government environments to avoid Mimikatz signatures. Legitimate use of comsvcs!MiniDump in user-context is essentially nil — pairing the exact ordinal #24 / 'MiniDump' verb with non-machine-account context yie

ucs.title: [LLM] Salt Typhoon Impacket-style WMIEXEC lateral execution (wmiprvse.exe → cmd.exe with SMB ADMIN$ output redirection)
ucs.description: Hunts the lateral-movement tradecraft tied to Salt Typhoon / Earth Estries' use of Impacket's wmiexec.py and atexec.py against telecom backbone hosts: WmiPrvSE.exe spawning cmd.exe with the characteristic '/Q /c ... 1> \\127.0.0.1\ADMIN$\__<timestamp> 2>&1' command shape, used to execute commands remotely over WMI and stage output back via SMB.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.process_id) as pid from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="WmiPrvSE.exe" Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") (Processes.process="*\\\\127.0.0.1\\ADMIN$*" OR Processes.process="*1> \\\\*\\ADMIN$*" OR Processes.process="*\\\\*\\C$\\__*" OR Processes.process="*2>&1*" Processes.process="*/Q /c*") NOT Processes.user IN ("*$") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rex field=cmdline "(?<staging>\\\\\\\\[^\\\\]+\\\\(?:ADMIN|C)\\$\\\\__\d{10,})" | eval salt_typhoon_uc="impacket_wmiexec" | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "WmiPrvSE.exe"
| where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
// Impacket wmiexec.py canonical shape: cmd.exe /Q /c <cmd> 1> \\127.0.0.1\ADMIN$\__<epoch> 2>&1
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)/Q\s+/c\s+.+1>\s*\\\\[^\\]+\\(ADMIN|C)\$\\__\d{8,}"
   or ProcessCommandLine has_all (@"\\127.0.0.1\ADMIN$", "2>&1")
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\\\\[^\\]+\\C\$\\__\d{10,}(?:\.\d+)?\s*2>&1"
| extend StagingShare = extract(@"(?i)(\\\\[^\\\s]+\\(?:ADMIN|C)\$\\__\d+(?:\.\d+)?)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          ChildProcess = FileName,
          ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          StagingShare,
          IntegrityLevel = ProcessIntegrityLevel,
          IsRemoteParent = InitiatingProcessTokenElevation
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceLogonEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType == "LogonSuccess"
    | where LogonType in ("Network","NetworkCleartext")
    | project DeviceName, AuthAccount = AccountName, AuthRemoteIP = RemoteIP,
              AuthTime = Timestamp, AuthLogonId = LogonId
  ) on $left.DeviceName == $right.DeviceName
| where AuthTime between (Timestamp - 5m .. Timestamp)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ChildCmd, StagingShare,
          AuthAccount, AuthRemoteIP, AuthTime
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\WmiPrvSE.exe"
| where NewProcessName endswith_cs "\\cmd.exe"
     or NewProcessName endswith_cs "\\powershell.exe"
     or NewProcessName endswith_cs "\\pwsh.exe"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)/Q\s+/c\s+.+1>\s*\\\\[^\\]+\\(ADMIN|C)\$\\__\d{8,}"
     or (CommandLine has @"\\127.0.0.1\ADMIN$" and CommandLine has "2>&1")
     or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\\\\[^\\]+\\C\$\\__\d{10,}(?:\.\d+)?\s*2>&1"
| extend StagingShare = extract(@"(?i)(\\\\[^\\\s]+\\(?:ADMIN|C)\$\\__\d+(?:\.\d+)?)", 1, CommandLine)
| join kind=leftouter (
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4624
    | where LogonType in (3, 8)               // Network / NetworkCleartext
    | project Computer, AuthAccount = TargetUserName, AuthIP = IpAddress,
              AuthTime = TimeGenerated, AuthLogonId = TargetLogonId
  ) on Computer
| where AuthTime between (TimeGenerated - 5m .. TimeGenerated)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          StagingShare, AuthAccount, AuthIP, AuthTime
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Salt Typhoon / Earth Estries operators are reported by Trend Micro, CISA, and Microsoft to use Impacket (wmiexec.py / atexec.py) extensively for hop-by-hop movement across telecom and ISP environments after initial GhostSpider / Demodex deployment. The wmiexec.py command shape — '/Q /c <cmd> 1> \\127.0.0.1\ADMIN$\__<epoch> 2>&1' executed under WmiPrvSE — is hard-coded in Impacket and almost never
